---
title: "c4ptur3-th3-fl4g"
tags:
  - fetus
---
- room link: https://tryhackme.com/room/c4ptur3th3fl4g
# T1: `Translation & Shifting`
## T1Q1:
```sh
c4n y0u c4p7u23 7h3 f149?
```
- ez
- `can you capture the flag?`
## T1Q2:
```sh
01101100 01100101 01110100 01110011 00100000 01110100 01110010 01111001 00100000 01110011 01101111 01101101 01100101 00100000 01100010 01101001 01101110 01100001 01110010 01111001 00100000 01101111 01110101 01110100 00100001
```
- `lets try some binary out!`
## T1Q3:
```sh
MJQXGZJTGIQGS4ZAON2XAZLSEBRW63LNN5XCA2LOEBBVIRRHOM======
```
- since its all uppercase and numbers its probably base32
- yup base32 `base32 is super common in CTF's`
## T1Q4:
```sh
RWFjaCBCYXNlNjQgZGlnaXQgcmVwcmVzZW50cyBleGFjdGx5IDYgYml0cyBvZiBkYXRhLg==
```
- base64 :v
- `Each Base64 digit represents exactly 6 bits of data.`
## T1Q5:
```sh
68 65 78 61 64 65 63 69 6d 61 6c 20 6f 72 20 62 61 73 65 31 36 3f
```
- hex probably?
- yeah hex `hexadecimal or base16?`
## T1Q6:
```sh
Ebgngr zr 13 cynprf!
```
- ROT13?
- yup ROT13. my spidy senses have grown xD `Rotate me 13 places!`
## T1Q7:
```sh
*@F DA:? >6 C:89E C@F?5 323J C:89E C@F?5 Wcf E:>6DX
```
- hmm this one is kinda weird
- oh ROT47 lol. shifts by ascii number 47 positions :3
- `You spin me right round baby right round (47 times)`
## T1Q8:
```sh
- . .-.. . -.-. --- -- -- ..- -. .. -.-. .- - .. --- -.
. -. -.-. --- -.. .. -. --.
```
- morse code obviously
- `TELECOMMUNICATION ENCODING`
## T1Q9:
```sh
85 110 112 97 99 107 32 116 104 105 115 32 66 67 68
```
- hmmm not hex. idk what this is. lets use [[cipher-identifier]] :D
- decimal code huh. noted. 
- `Unpack this BCD`
## T1Q10:
```sh fold title:"skibidihh"

```
- weird but ok. says base64. got what seems like morse code after decoding:
```sh
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- .---- .---- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- .---- ----- .----
----- ----- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- ----- .---- .---- .---- .---- .----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- ----- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- .---- ----- .----
----- ----- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- .---- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- .---- ----- ----- -----
----- ----- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- .---- ----- -----
----- ----- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----

----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----

----- .---- .---- ----- ----- .---- ----- .----
----- ----- .---- ----- ----- ----- ----- -----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- ----- .---- .----
----- .---- .---- ----- ----- .---- ----- .----
```
- lets decode this one too 
- aaand we get binary
```sh
01100110 01100101 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100000 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100101 00100000 01100010 01101000 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100100 00100000 01100010 01100001 00100000 01100000 01011111 01101000 00100000 01101000 01100110 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100110 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100000 00100000 01100010 01100001 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100101 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100011 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100100 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100100 00100000 01100010 01100001 00100000 01101000 01100110 00100000 01100010 01100001 00100000 01101000 01100111 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100100 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100101 00100000 01100010 01100001 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100101 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100011 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100100 00100000 01101000 01101000 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100110 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100100 00100000 01100000 01011111 01100000 00100000 01100000 01100000 01100011 00100000 01100011 01100101 00100000 01100011 01100101 00100000 01100011 01100101
```
- we get weird stuff from this 
```sh
fe `_` ``e bh ``d ba `_h hf `_f `_` ba ``e `_c `_d ``d ba hf ba hg `_d ``e ba ``e ``c `_d hh `_f `_d `_` ``c ce ce ce
```
- weird = ROT47 so lets try that:
- yup. got decimal code from that
```sh
76 101 116 39 115 32 109 97 107 101 32 116 104 105 115 32 97 32 98 105 116 32 116 114 105 99 107 105 101 114 46 46 46
```
- finally -\_\_-
- `Let's make this a bit trickier...`
# T2: `Spectrograms`
- downloaded the `wav` file. lets put it into Sonic Visualiser and see what comes up: <br>
![[c4ptur3-th3-fl4gSpectrogramSS.png]]
- nice. `Super Secret Message`
# T3: `Steganography`
- downloaded the image <br>
![[c4ptur3-th3-fl4gStegoSteg.jpg.png]]
- lets see what this stego is hiding :D
```sh
╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[0]─[2784]
╰─[:)] # steghide --extract -sf stegosteg_1559008553457.jpg 
Enter passphrase: 
wrote extracted data to "steganopayload2248.txt".
```
- `steganopayload2248.txt`:
```txt
SpaghettiSteg#
```
# T4: `Security through obscurity`:
- downloaded the task file: <br>
![[c4ptur3-th3-fl4gMeme.png]]
- hell yeah i love memes. lets do it
## T4Q1: `Download and get 'inside' the file. What is the first filename & extension?`
- "first" file eh. lets do [[binwalk]] and see whats in there
```sh
╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[0]─[2793]
╰─[:)] # binwalk meme_1559010886025.jpg 

DECIMAL       HEXADECIMAL     DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
0             0x0             JPEG image data, JFIF standard 1.01
30            0x1E            TIFF image data, big-endian, offset of first image directory: 8
74407         0x122A7         RAR archive data, version 5.x
74478         0x122EE         PNG image, 147 x 37, 8-bit/color RGBA, non-interlaced
74629         0x12385         Zlib compressed data, default compression

```
- hmmm lets extract that shit 
```sh
╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[3]─[2796]
╰─[:(] # binwalk -e meme_1559010886025.jpg --run-as=root

DECIMAL       HEXADECIMAL     DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
74407         0x122A7         RAR archive data, version 5.x
74629         0x12385         Zlib compressed data, default compression

WARNING: One or more files failed to extract: either no utility was found or it's unimplemented

╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[0]─[2797]
╰─[:)] # ll
total 140K
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  79K May 27  2019 meme_1559010886025.jpg
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4.0K Jul 18 22:49 _meme_1559010886025.jpg.extracted
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root   13 Jul 18 22:42 steganopayload2248.txt
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  52K May 27  2019 stegosteg_1559008553457.jpg
╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[0]─[2798]
╰─[:)] # cd _meme_1559010886025.jpg.extracted 
╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g/_meme_1559010886025.jpg.extracted]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[0]─[2799]
╰─[:)] # ll
total 48K
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5.6K Jul 18 22:49 122A7.rar
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  22K Jul 18 22:49 12385
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5.4K Jul 18 22:49 12385.zlib
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5.5K May 27  2019 hackerchat.png
```
- four files nice
- `hackerchat.png`
## T4Q2: `Get inside the archive and inspect the file carefully. Find the hidden text.`
- ok lets unrar that rar file
```sh
╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g/_meme_1559010886025.jpg.extracted]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[0]─[2815]
╰─[:)] # unrar x 122A7.rar 

UNRAR 7.11 freeware      Copyright (c) 1993-2025 Alexander Roshal


Extracting from 122A7.rar


Would you like to replace the existing file hackerchat.png
  5562 bytes, modified on 2019-05-27 22:30
with a new one
  5562 bytes, modified on 2019-05-27 22:30

[Y]es, [N]o, [A]ll, n[E]ver, [R]ename, [Q]uit Y

Extracting  hackerchat.png                                            OK 
All OK
╭─[~/projects/c4ptur3-th3-fl4g/_meme_1559010886025.jpg.extracted]─[root@DEMONDAYZ]─[0]─[2816]
╰─[:)] # ll
total 48K
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5.6K Jul 18 22:49 122A7.rar
-rwx------ 1 root root  22K Jul 18 22:49 12385
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5.4K Jul 18 22:49 12385.zlib
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 5.5K May 27  2019 hackerchat.png
```
- damn more nested binwalk stuff -\_\_-
- got another file called `97.zlib`. idk how to decompress that actually. lets look it up. 
- tried it with [[zlib-flate]]. didnt work. lets do strings again on this new one to see if theres anything in it
- yup there it is -,- that was a total wild goose chase
- `AHH_YOU_FOUND_ME!`
